text.title: GraphQL API Design Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed when applying graphql-api-design. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix keyed to the methodology.

antipattern.symptom: createOrder returns Boolean; six months later, schema cannot communicate which fields had validation errors.
antipattern.root-cause: Author shortcut payload type; later additions break clients.
antipattern.fix: Add CreateOrderPayload type with order + userErrors. Migrate clients; deprecate scalar return; remove after grace window.

antipattern.symptom: Each resolver does its own scope check; an audit finds two resolvers missing the check entirely.
antipattern.fix: Move auth to @auth directive applied in SDL. Run an audit script that lists every protected field; reviewer signs off on directive coverage.

antipattern.symptom: Some lists use Connection/Edge; others use plain arrays with offset args; clients keep two pagination code paths.
antipattern.root-cause: Naming convention not enforced from the start.
antipattern.fix: Migrate all paginated lists to Connection. Run schema lint with relay-compatible rule.

antipattern.symptom: Relation `author: User` resolves on `int` id leaked from Postgres; later migration to UUID breaks clients.
antipattern.root-cause: Storage detail leaked into the schema.
antipattern.fix: Wrap with opaque ID type (Relay global ID). Server decodes; storage detail is hidden.
